page: Amazon Web Services m3.xlarge is a general-purpose, third-generation virtual server instance powered by the Xen hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon E5-2670 v2 processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The instance is configured with 4 dedicated virtual CPUs (vCPUs) mapped to 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It includes 15.0 GB of system memory, providing 3.75 GB of RAM per core, alongside an L3 cache of 25 MB. Storage is bundled with a local 80 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks position the m3.xlarge in the lower tiers of modern cloud compute resources. In stress-ng testing, both single-core and multi-core CPU performance fall into the bottom 25% category. Similarly, Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores are rated as poor, landing in the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth testing shows a read-only small block cached speed of 186.58 GB/sec and a large block uncached speed of 26.71 GB/sec, which are weak relative to newer hardware generations. However, the Passmark memory score achieves an average rating, representing a stable middle-tier performance for memory operations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m3.xlarge offers a legacy hardware profile with dedicated compute resources and local NVMe SSD storage, though its raw performance-per-resource ratio is lower than contemporary instance families. The instance is best suited for legacy general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, light database operations, and development or testing environments that require dedicated CPU allocation and local SSD storage without demanding high-performance compute or memory throughput.
description: Amazon Web Services m3.xlarge is a general-purpose, third-generation virtual server configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon E5-2670 v2 processor running at 2.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via the Xen hypervisor, it features 15.0 GB of system memory and a bundled 80 GB local NVMe SSD. Network connectivity is established at a baseline speed of 1 Gbps. Benchmark results indicate that the instance delivers weak to poor performance across CPU-intensive tasks, software compilation, and cryptography, though its Passmark memory score remains average. Given its legacy hardware profile, the m3.xlarge is qualitatively cost-effective primarily for steady-state, low-intensity workloads. It is best suited for basic web serving, development environments, and light database operations that benefit from dedicated CPU allocation and local SSD storage without requiring high-throughput modern compute resources.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m3.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual server featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs, 15 GB of RAM, and a bundled 80 GB local NVMe SSD, suitable for legacy workloads and basic web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m3.xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 4 vCPUs, 15 GB RAM, and an 80 GB local NVMe SSD for steady-state legacy workloads.
tagline: A legacy general-purpose virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and local NVMe SSD storage for steady-state workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E5-2670 v2 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 15 GB RAM.
Bundled 80 GB local NVMe SSD storage and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
Average memory performance suitable for steady-state, low-intensity operations.
Legacy hardware profile best suited for basic web serving and development environments.
Dedicated CPU allocation utilizing the Xen hypervisor on x86_64 architecture.
